title: fully hosted SaaS control plane, authentication, teams, collaboration, and persistence
trigger: the generic local and CI runner contract is stable and the redacted hosted-envelope gate passes

title: Git, GitHub, or Graphite transport and review replacement
trigger: the submitted semantic layer proves a transport limitation that cannot be handled by an adapter

title: Nx, Turborepo, TypeScript, and other BlueprintProvider adapters
trigger: the GraphReFly flagship is submission-ready and one external provider has a concrete fixture and fidelity contract

title: MCP server, GitHub App, hosted CI, and required-check adapters
trigger: the canonical CLI gate is stable and an approved integration can execute the same deterministic contract

title: automatic semantic approval and merge
trigger: post-MVP evidence defines policy, identity, authorization, rollback, and audit requirements

title: additional demo scenarios and production persistence
trigger: the flagship scenario is complete, judgeable, and submission-ready

title: DAG stacks, merge-parent selection, and partial-order review
trigger: the generic linear repository gate passes and the domain contract can add partial-order ancestry without changing WorkUnit identity

title: Stack-assisted semantic planning and plan-to-work-unit creation
trigger: fulfilled by PRODUCT-VERIFY and D31; implement only through PRODUCT-SEMANTIC-0 to PRODUCT-SEMANTIC-VERIFY

title: mutual exclusion, reservations, and concurrent-work ownership
trigger: real PR integration evidence proves that optimistic three-way semantic validation cannot safely coordinate a demonstrated workflow without preventive ownership

title: pull-request textual and semantic integration conflicts
trigger: fulfilled by D37 and PRODUCT-COLLAB after the semantic, CI and hosted contracts can bind one exact three-way candidate

title: enforceable actor-specific source and capability credentials
trigger: real usage demonstrates that existing accepted WorkUnit source and capability validation at PR time cannot provide a required actor-specific authorization boundary

title: evidence-backed revert, rollback planning, and dependent-stack recovery
trigger: activated by D44 and PRODUCT-ROLLBACK after DAG lineage, immutable bindings, and policy-aware inverse or compensating changes became independently verifiable without treating Git revert success as semantic recovery

title: deadline-safe product polish after generic MVP proof
trigger: PRODUCT-1 and the first refreshed submission information are complete with enough time remaining before BW-D1 to preserve a final evidence and submission refresh

title: exact per-revision dependency installation, immutable package caching, and monorepo package selection
trigger: a real repository requires historical dependency semantics that cannot be evaluated with one compatible exact review runtime or selects different workspace packages across the range

title: optional JSON, YAML, and TOML repository configuration authoring formats
trigger: the strict JSON v1 onboarding contract has user acceptance and additional formats can normalize into the same validated repository configuration without creating divergent runtime semantics

title: optional global grfs launcher that delegates to a repository-local engine, plus zero-install pnpm dlx usage
trigger: the repository-pinned install and .git/grfs local-state contract have user acceptance and launcher resolution can fail closed instead of silently changing engine versions

title: standalone and line-level review comments with immutable blob, path and diff-position anchors
trigger: durable per-commit review decisions have user acceptance and comment rebinding can mark stale anchors without silently moving feedback after a rebase

title: attributed manual merge resolution, octopus joins, multiple heads and larger DAG bounds
trigger: verified PRODUCT-DAG usage finds a compatible repository whose required workflow cannot be represented by one base, one head, at most 64 objects, width eight and binary clean transport joins, and supplies concrete intent, scope, parent-selection, performance and recovery evidence for the smallest expansion

title: merge-queue rebase and squash identity preservation
trigger: verified clean-merge merge-group usage identifies a repository that requires GitHub queue rebase or squash and supplies an independently verifiable mapping from rewritten commits to accepted Plan and WorkUnit identities without provider or message heuristics

title: cross-Plan semantic dependency and coordinated recovery
trigger: real multi-Plan merge-group usage demonstrates that one independently accepted Plan must intentionally depend on another Plan in the same group and supplies an ownership, approval, ordering, invalidation and recovery contract that cannot be represented as graph integration alone

title: portable and hosted DAG and merge-group evidence sync
trigger: repository-local multi-Plan DAG evidence is verified and an explicit redaction profile, authorization, quota, retention and degraded-mode design can extend D36 without uploading source, unredacted Blueprints or making hosted availability part of the required verdict
